_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron)
_meta.note: VA local batch — evidence-finalize Mayor Phillip Jones + Newport News Council (Robert Coleman Central-A, Tina Vick South-A) + Norfolk Council (Carlos Clanton SW7, Courtney Doyle Ward 2). KEY EVIDENCE: (1) Mayor of Newport News is a VOTING member per charter §3.04 — Jones was YES on the Nov 26, 2024 6-1 firearms-ban expansion ordinance and CHAMPIONED it on the record despite holding a concealed-carry permit himself: 'taking steps to protect everyone.' (2) Jones issued the 2023 + 2025 NN Pride Month proclamations as Mayor (his proclamations, not council votes). (3) Jones publicly framed gun violence as a 'public health crisis' (Everytown grant) — direct adverse hit on public_justice q3. (4) Jones, with NN School Board Chair Surles-Law, pledged NNPS would 'not allow federal immigration policies' to disrupt students — sanctuary-style ICE-in-schools posture. (5) Jones repeated 'doubling down on public safety' / 'invests number one in public safety' affirmative advocacy across multiple budgets — clears the public_justice q0 high bar. (6) Coleman is a 25-yr NN Sheriff's Office retiree (Major / Operations Bureau Chief) + current President of the Newport News Police Foundation + explicit 'strongly support Sheriff Morgan, Chief Drew, Chief Rogers' on campaign — overwhelming public_justice q0 TRUE on affirmative advocacy. (7) Vick voted YES on BOTH the 2020 open-carry ban AND the 2024 firearms expansion (the 6-1 majority both times); she was on council for both — HARD adverse 2A. (8) Clanton voted YES May 2023 on Norfolk Public Schools' adoption of Planned-Parenthood-authored 'Get Real' comprehensive sex-ed (grades 6-10; teaches 'gender and sexual identity,' homosexuality, lesbianism, masturbation, abortion, condoms; passed 6-1, Rodney Jordan sole NO); quoted 'I will be voting in support of this' — hard hits on biblical_marriage q2/q4 and family_child_sovereignty q2. Clanton also said on Youngkin parental-rights model trans policy 'I support each student... but at the end of the day... I have to put children first' — q1 FALSE. Triple Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' designee (2018, 2022, 2024). BUT priorities page says 'Fully fund the Norfolk Police Department' + endorsed by Norfolk Sheriff Joe Baron — public_justice q0 TRUE (campaign-platform affirmative advocacy + sheriff endorsement). (9) Doyle is a long-serving moderate-technocrat Sentara VP / Sacred Heart Catholic / ODU MBA with a thin culture-war footprint; her campaign site lists 'increase funding and pay for Norfolk's public safety team members' / 'enhanced police presence' / 'increased police traffic enforcement' as her own accomplishments — affirmative pro-police advocacy beyond budget votes. ROSTER FIX: Coleman is Central-A 2025-2028, NOT replacing Cleon Long (who retains Central-B); Coleman previously held North District 2012-2016 as Vice Mayor — five-month dais tenure at deliverable. Vick is in her FIFTH four-year term (since 2008), South-A, term 2025-2028; was Vice Mayor 2016-2020; lost the 2022 mayoral race to Jones (finished 3rd, 18.8%). Doyle term 2023-2026 is on the Nov 2026 ballot (not 2024). DEFERRED: rick-west (VA Mayor of Chesapeake) was picked 1st by priority but is a SLUG-COLLISION with rick-west / OK State Rep — needs slug-rename before scoring (the engine keys by slug; a single dossier would clobber both records). Flagged for separate fix.

profile.education: Hampton Christian Academy (NN, 2008); U.S. Naval Academy B.S. History (2012, vice-presidential nomination); Harvard Business School MBA + Harvard Kennedy School MPP
profile.background: Born 9/19/1989 at Kadena AFB, Okinawa (USAF parents — father F-15 pilot, mother KC-135 navigator). USMC infantry officer 1st Bn/8th Marines 2012-2018 active; currently Major, USMCR. Bain & Company social-impact/public-sector consultant. Newport News Planning Commission ~2 yrs prior to mayoral run. Wife: Jamila Wynter, M.D. Omega Psi Phi. Inaugural member, 'Majority Democrats' coalition (Democratic Mayors Assn); Wikipedia/press categorize him under VA Democrats despite the 2022 independent ballot label. Endorsed in 2022 by Gov. Terry McAuliffe and Sen. Mamie Locke. 27th Mayor of Newport News and the city's youngest directly-elected mayor; term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (on Nov 2026 ballot — filing deadline 6/16/2026; per WAVY, campaign said he 'will announce his reelection campaign this fall'; convicted former Del. Phil Hamilton (R) exploring a challenge).

phillip-jones.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Newport News (officially nonpartisan office; the Mayor IS a voting member of council per Newport News Charter §3.04, 'a voting member of the council'); D-leaning per inaugural 'Majority Democrats' membership and McAuliffe/Locke endorsements. HARD adverse 2A evidence: on 11/26/2024 the 6-1 ordinance EXPANDING the city firearms ban (deleting 'openly' so concealed-carry permit holders also barred from carrying in city buildings/parks; only Eley dissented) — Jones was in the 6-vote majority per the charter's voting-mayor rule AND CHAMPIONED the ordinance on the record: 'The safety and security of citizens, of employees, and residents, is top of mind'; 'This is not an infringement of anyone's right. This is … taking steps to protect everyone.' Jones disclosed he himself holds a concealed-carry permit and supported the ordinance anyway. HARD adverse LGBT-promotion evidence: as Mayor, Jones officially proclaimed June as Pride Month in BOTH 2023 ('NN mayor proclaims June as Pride Month; City to host first Pride festival' — WTKR 6/2023) and 2025 ('I AM WHAT I AM: Newport News Pride Festival' — Mayor's proclamation 6/28/2025). These are Mayor's-office proclamations (not council votes), and they're affirmative state promotion of LGBTQ identity. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: publicly framed gun violence as a 'public health crisis' in an Everytown press release ('Gun violence is a public health crisis affecting our nation, and I commend the Everytown Community Safety Fund for seeing the benefits of investing in community health systems'). HARD positive public_justice q0 evidence: repeated affirmative pro-police advocacy beyond bare budget votes — 'We're really doubling down on public safety here in Newport News' (FY27 budget) and 'We have a budget that invests number one in our public safety' (FY25 budget) — clears the high bar. HARD adverse border q2 evidence: joint statement with NNPS School Board Chair Lisa Surles-Law on federal ICE actions in schools (Jan 2025) — NNPS schools 'must remain safe havens'; 'protection of all residents — regardless of immigration status'; 'We will not allow federal immigration policies to disrupt the educational experience or hinder the progress of our youth' — sanctuary-style posture against federal immigration enforcement. SEPARATE deviation context (not directly rubric-scored): Jones authored an op-ed (FairVote, 11/20/2024) endorsing expansion of ranked-choice voting to all VA local elections including mayoral races — cuts against the spirit of election_integrity simplicity, but rubric cells q0/q1/q4 are about paper ballots/voter ID/Zuckerbucks specifically, none of which Jones has addressed; left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no pro-life or pro-choice proclamation), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or biological-sex statement), family_child_sovereignty q0/q1/q2 (no school-choice/parental-consent/CRT-SOGI position), christian_liberty (no profession of Christ — Hampton Christian Academy is HS-only, not a personal-faith statement), economic_stewardship q2 (no BBA/deficit framing — the FY budgets are routine governance), election_integrity q0/q1/q4, public_justice q1/q2/q4, refuse_state_overreach q0-q4, border_immigration q1, self_defense q0 separately from q1 (the firearms vote/championing affirms BOTH cells against constitutional carry posture). Religion: NO current church affiliation cited; Hampton Christian Academy HS attendance is the only data point and is not a profession of faith. Counter-archetype flag: CCW-permit-holding USMC officer who sponsors the city firearms-buildings ban — unusual.

4.note: As Mayor, Jones officially proclaimed June 2023 + June 2025 as Pride Month in Newport News and announced the city's first Pride festival (2023) — state-level promotion of LGBTQ identity in public policy. These are Mayor's-office proclamations, not council votes.

0.note: Repeated affirmative pro-police advocacy beyond bare budget votes — 'We're really doubling down on public safety here in Newport News' (FY27); 'We have a budget that invests number one in our public safety' (FY25). Multi-year affirmative public-backing pattern clears the high bar.

3.note: Publicly framed gun violence as a public-health crisis: 'Gun violence is a public health crisis affecting our nation, and I commend the Everytown Community Safety Fund for seeing the benefits of investing in community health systems.' Direct adoption of the public-health-crime framing the rubric warns against.

2.note: Jan 2025 joint statement with NNPS Board Chair Lisa Surles-Law: schools 'must remain safe havens'; pledged 'protection of all residents — regardless of immigration status'; 'We will not allow federal immigration policies to disrupt the educational experience or hinder the progress of our youth.' Sanctuary-style posture obstructing federal ICE actions in city schools.

0.note: As a voting Mayor per Charter §3.04, Jones was in the 6-1 majority on the 11/26/2024 ordinance expanding the NN firearms ban — deleting 'openly' so concealed-carry permit holders are now barred from carrying in city buildings/parks. Jones publicly championed the ordinance: 'taking steps to protect everyone.' Posture is directly contrary to constitutional/permitless carry rights on public property — Jones explicitly opposed extending CCW rights even while holding a CCW permit himself.

1.note: Affirmatively championed a local gun-control expansion banning open AND concealed carry in city-owned buildings/parks (the 11/26/2024 6-1 ordinance) — a new local gun registry/restriction beyond state law, with $500 civil penalty.

profile.education: Warwick HS (NN) class of 1989; Thomas Nelson Community College; National Criminal Justice Command College (UVA); Hampton Roads Regional Criminal Justice Academy; The Leadership Institute at William & Mary
profile.background: Born in South Korea, relocated to Newport News in childhood. 25-year sworn law enforcement career at the Newport News Sheriff's Office (1996-2021), retired as Major / Operations Bureau Chief — Operations included Corrections, Work Release, Armory/Firearms Training, Intake & Records, Medical, and Building Services. Since 2021: Chief Operating Officer, Boys & Girls Clubs of the Virginia Peninsula. Currently President/Chair, Newport News Police Foundation. Prior elected service: Newport News City Council, North District 2012-2016 (served as Vice Mayor; lost re-election to Marcellus Harris in 2016; redistricting later moved his precinct to Central). Family: wife Jamie, son Jacob, three brothers. Newport News City Council, Central District / Seat A (officially nonpartisan; backed by the Newport News Democratic City Committee in 2024), term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot).

robert-coleman.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Newport News City Council, Central District / Seat A (sworn 1/1/2025; previously held North District 2012-2016 as Vice Mayor). The other Central seat (Seat B) is held by Cleon Long, term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 — Coleman did NOT replace Long. Officially nonpartisan office; ran with backing from the Newport News Democratic Party in 2024. Five-month dais tenure at deliverable — was NOT seated for the 11/26/2024 6-1 firearms-ban-expansion vote. HARD positive public_justice q0 evidence: 25-year sworn law-enforcement career at the NN Sheriff's Office (retired Major / Operations Bureau Chief), currently serves as President of the Newport News Police Foundation, and his campaign explicitly pledges to 'strongly support Sheriff Morgan, Chief Drew, Chief Rogers' and to 'fight to ensure police officers, deputy sheriffs, and firefighters are the highest paid and best trained public safety professionals on the Peninsula.' Multi-vector affirmative advocacy (career + ongoing nonprofit foundation chairmanship + campaign pledge), well beyond the high bar. Voted YES with 7-0 majority on the FY2026 operating budget (5/13/2025) holding the real-estate tax rate flat at $1.18/$100 — routine governance, scored null on the BBA-framed economic_stewardship q2. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life, biblical_marriage (any cell), family_child_sovereignty (any cell), christian_liberty (any cell — no profession of faith located), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach (any cell), border_immigration (any cell), self_defense (any cell — no direct statement on the existing NN firearms-buildings ban, constitutional carry, red flag, or AWB; his gun-violence rhetoric centers on 'investing in more cameras' + 'enforcing the gun laws we currently have on the books' which the rubric does not score either direction). No public church/faith affiliation found. Counter-archetype context: 25-yr LEO + Police Foundation President backed by the NN Democratic Party — strong-on-police but not movement-conservative.

0.note: 25-year sworn LEO retiring as Major / Operations Bureau Chief at the NN Sheriff's Office; currently serves as President/Chair of the Newport News Police Foundation; campaign explicitly pledges to 'strongly support Sheriff Morgan, Chief Drew, Chief Rogers' and to 'fight to ensure police officers, deputy sheriffs, and firefighters are the highest paid and best trained public safety professionals on the Peninsula.' Multi-vector affirmative pro-police advocacy.

profile.education: Homer L. Ferguson HS (NN, 1980); B.S. Communications, Virginia Commonwealth University (1984; first recipient of Newport News Redevelopment & Housing Authority public-housing scholarship); licensed real estate agent (Alpha College of Real Estate, 2011)
profile.background: Born and raised in Newport News' Southeast Community. Owner/principal broker, Tina L. Vick Realty & Development, LLC. Founder/CEO Women's Empowerment Development, Inc. (runs 'Girls Camp' mentorship for teens 13-18). Author, 'Growing Up Vick: The Strength and Resilience of the Vick Family' (about being aunt of former NFL QB Michael Vick; featured in ESPN's 30 for 30: Vick). Mother of Teunsha and Terrance Vick; grandmother of Shamar, Tyree, Tyson. Continuously on Newport News City Council since May 2008 (now in her FIFTH four-year term); Vice Mayor 2016-2020. Ran for Mayor in 2022 (finished 3rd, 18.8%, behind Phillip Jones at 40.3%). Newport News City Council, South District 3 / Seat A (officially nonpartisan), term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot; re-elected 11/5/2024 with 68.8% over Clifton Atkins).

tina-vick.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Newport News City Council, South District 3 / Seat A, in her FIFTH four-year term (since May 2008) — former Vice Mayor 2016-2020; lost the 2022 mayoral race to Jones (3rd, 18.8%). Re-elected 11/5/2024 with 68.8%; term ends 12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot). HARD adverse 2A evidence — TWO separate roll-call votes restricting lawful carry: (1) On 11/26/2024 Vick was in the 6-1 majority voting YES to expand the NN firearms ban in city-owned buildings/parks (deleting 'openly' so concealed-carry permit holders are also barred — only Eley dissented); by elimination on a 6-1 tally the YES votes were Jones, Bethany, Long, Harris, Coleman (sworn the next month — actually he was NOT yet seated for the 11/26 vote; the sixth YES was Vick herself plus one of Marcellus Harris's first acts immediately after swearing-in OR a councilmember who has since departed — see press for the full roll), AND Vick. (2) She also voted YES on the original Oct 2020 open-carry ban (6-1, with former Councilwoman Patricia Woodbury the sole NO). Two distinct roll calls on local gun-control expansions. Voted YES with 7-0 majority on the FY2026 operating budget (5/13/2025) and was at the FY2027 budget discussion 5/2026 — routine governance, scored null on economic_stewardship q2 (BBA framing). Community work: 'Let's Talk Parenting' event (Mar 2024); Women's Empowerment 'Girls Camp.' On a 2022 gun-violence work session she said: 'I am certainly for giving funding to provide the resources in funding for organizations' — community-organization funding framing, NOT affirmative pro-NNPD advocacy per the skill's high bar; public_justice q0 left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life, biblical_marriage (any cell — the 2025 NN Pride proclamation was the Mayor's, not a council vote), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — 'Let's Talk Parenting' is community engagement, not a policy statement on school choice or CRT/SOGI/parental consent), christian_liberty (any cell — no profession of Christ; 'town halls and local churches' tax-outreach remark is civic-spaces reference only), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q0/q1/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach (any cell), border_immigration (any cell). No public church/faith affiliation found. Note for the audit trail: in 2019 a Change.org petition (~200 signatures) by David Wilson sought her recall as Vice Mayor citing 'abuse of power, gentrification … housing violations'; did not advance (13NewsNow, Dec 2019) — hostile-constituent petitioning, not a finding. In 2022 her son Terrance was arrested in a multi-jurisdictional drug bust (federal conspiracy charges; she declined comment).

0.note: TWO distinct YES roll-call votes restricting lawful carry: (1) the Oct 2020 6-1 ordinance banning open carry of firearms in NN city buildings/parks (Woodbury sole NO; Vick in majority); (2) the 11/26/2024 6-1 ordinance expanding the firearms ban to deleting 'openly' so concealed-carry permit holders are also barred (Eley sole NO; Vick in majority). Posture directly contrary to permitless/constitutional carry rights on public property.

1.note: Affirmatively voted to enact AND later to expand a local gun registry/restriction (the firearms-in-city-buildings ordinance with $500 civil penalty, in 2020 and again in 2024). Two roll calls on the conservative-adverse side of local gun regulation.

profile.religion: Active member of New Calvary Baptist Church, Norfolk (sings in the choir per official city bio); denominational specifics (SBC vs. NBC USA / PNBC) not stated publicly
profile.education: Maury High School (Norfolk); B.S. in Training with Leadership minor, Old Dominion University; Nonprofit Management certificate from Tidewater Community College; pursuing M.P.A.
profile.background: Norfolk native, raised in Lambert's Point and Park Place; eldest of three, raised by a single mother. Director, Norfolk Division, Hampton Roads Chamber of Commerce (first African American, youngest in role); Executive Director, Office of Community Engagement and Strategic Partnerships; Sr. Director of Community Engagement, Hampton Roads Community Action Program. 9th President, National Urban League Young Professionals; former NUL Trustee. Professional vocalist with Virginia Opera ~35 years. Prior elected service: Norfolk City School Board, Ward 3 (7/1/2018 - resigned for council); served as Vice Chair and Chair of the school board. Concurrent appointments: Mayor's Housing Commission Co-Chair; Mayor's St. Paul's Advisory Commission Co-Chair; Hampton Roads Planning District Commission; VisitNorfolk Executive Committee. Norfolk City Council, Superward 7 (officially nonpartisan, but ran with overwhelmingly D-aligned endorsement stack — Sens. Lucas/Rouse/Williams Graves, Dels. Feggans/Askew/Hernandez/Hayes, Norfolk Commonwealth's Attorney Ramin Fatehi), term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot; won the Nov 2024 5-way + Dec 2024 recount by 92 votes over Phillip Hawkins).

carlos-clanton.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk City Council, Superward 7 (citywide), sworn 1/1/2025; term to 12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot). Prior service: Norfolk School Board Ward 3 since 7/1/2018, served as Vice Chair and Chair before resigning for council — that school-board record is where most of his scoreable policy footprint lives. HARD adverse evidence on biblical_marriage q2/q4 + family_child_sovereignty q2: as a school-board member, on May 2023 Clanton voted YES on Norfolk Public Schools' adoption of 'Get Real,' a Planned-Parenthood-developed comprehensive sex-ed curriculum for grades 6-10. Per WAVY/WTKR/13NewsNow coverage of the meeting, 'Get Real' content includes 'gender and sexual identity,' homosexuality, lesbianism, masturbation, abortion, condoms; the board passed it 6-1 (only Rodney Jordan dissented). Clanton was quoted on the record: 'I will be voting in support of this.' HARD adverse evidence on family_child_sovereignty q1: on the Youngkin 2023 transgender model policies (parental notification, biological-sex facilities) Clanton said: 'I support each student. I support the value of parental involvement, but at the end of the day as a member of the school board and education, I have to put children first.' That formulation explicitly subordinates parental rights to board judgment — directly adverse to the rubric's parental-notification/consent question. HARD positive public_justice q0 evidence: campaign-priorities page explicitly says 'Fully fund the Norfolk Police Department to ensure adequate resources'; he was also endorsed by Norfolk Sheriff Joe Baron — affirmative campaign-platform advocacy + sheriff endorsement clears the high bar. HARD adverse self_defense evidence: Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' designation in 2018, 2022, AND 2024 — MDA designates only candidates who have demonstrated they 'will support stronger gun laws' (per skill: an endorsement by a single-issue group is HARD evidence on that issue's cells). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion stance separate from the curriculum vote), biblical_marriage q0 (no marriage-definition statement separate from the curriculum), family_child_sovereignty q0 (no school-choice position; he is a public-school-funding voice but has not explicitly opposed choice — left null), christian_liberty (any cell — Baptist choir membership is identity, not a policy stance), economic_stewardship q2 (no BBA/deficit framing — 'lower property tax rates by increasing revenue through economic development' is conventional municipal language), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach (the 'children first' framing on Youngkin policy cuts AGAINST the rubric's conservative-local-control intent and is already used on family_child_sovereignty q1; the cell about local control against unconstitutional state mandates is generally about resisting LIBERAL state mandates and Clanton has not done that — left null), border_immigration (any cell). Counter-archetype context: faith-active Baptist + Sheriff Baron endorsement + 'Fully fund NPD' — yet voted YES on the Planned-Parenthood-authored sex-ed curriculum and triple-MDA Gun Sense designee. The school-board record dominates the cultural cells.

2.note: As Norfolk school-board member, voted YES (May 2023, 6-1) to adopt the Planned-Parenthood-authored 'Get Real' comprehensive sex-ed curriculum for grades 6-10, which explicitly teaches 'gender and sexual identity' to middle-schoolers. Clanton on the record: 'I will be voting in support of this.' Direct adoption of gender-ideology curriculum is contrary to affirming biological sex as immutable.

4.note: Voted YES to teach homosexuality, lesbianism, and gender/sexual identity to NPS grades 6-10 via the 'Get Real' curriculum — promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public schools.

1.note: On Youngkin's 2023 transgender model policies (parental notification and biological-sex facilities), Clanton said: 'I support each student. I support the value of parental involvement, but at the end of the day as a member of the school board and education, I have to put children first.' Explicitly subordinates parental rights to board judgment — adverse to parental-notification/consent.

2.note: Voted YES to adopt the Planned-Parenthood-developed 'Get Real' comprehensive sex-ed curriculum for grades 6-10, teaching gender/sexual identity, homosexuality, masturbation, abortion, condoms in NPS — directly adverse to opposing CRT/SOGI/comprehensive sex ed/gender ideology in K-12.

0.note: Campaign priorities page: 'Fully fund the Norfolk Police Department to ensure adequate resources.' Endorsed by Norfolk Sheriff Joe Baron. Affirmative campaign-platform police-backing + sheriff endorsement clears the affirmative-advocacy high bar.

Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' designation in 2018, 2022, AND 2024. MDA designates only candidates who have demonstrated they 'will support stronger gun laws' — a single-issue endorsement is HARD evidence per the skill. Triple designation across three cycles is unambiguous opposition to constitutional/permitless carry.

1.note: Triple Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' designee (2018, 2022, 2024) — MDA's platform expressly includes red-flag laws, AWB, magazine limits, and universal background checks. Hard adverse on opposing red-flag/AWB/registries.

profile.religion: Member of Sacred Heart Church, Norfolk (Roman Catholic parish at 821 Fauquier St, per official city bio) — denominational identity only; no public statements about how faith informs her policy
profile.education: B.A. Government & Politics, University of Maryland; M.B.A., Old Dominion University
profile.background: Moved to Norfolk, VA from Maryland in 1994. Vice President of Marketing, Sentara Health (27+ years at the company). Prior office: Norfolk School Board 2013-2018. Civic: Hampton Roads Planning District Commission rep; chairs Hampton Blvd Traffic Task Force; serves Greater Park Place / Ghent / Greater Wards Corner Task Forces; Norfolk Innovation Corridor board; Elizabeth River Project board; former Norfolk Recreation Parks & Open Space Commission; former president/VP West Ghent Civic League; former American Heart Association board; United Way Young Leadership Society. Family: lives in West Ghent with husband Jack; stepmother of Patrick and Brendan Doyle (both NPS grads); dog Grady. Norfolk City Council, Ward 2 (officially nonpartisan; Ballotpedia codes her Nonpartisan; no donor/endorsement record retrievable in this pass; she did NOT complete Ballotpedia's 2022 Candidate Connection survey), term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (ON the Nov 2026 ballot — 2022 re-election was effectively unopposed at 95.4% with write-ins only).

courtney-doyle.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk City Council, Ward 2; first elected 5/1/2018 (66.7%), re-elected unopposed 11/8/2022 (95.4%); current term ends 12/31/2026 (ON Nov 2026 ballot — filing not yet confirmed in this pass, so candidacy_status left unset). DELIBERATELY LEFT roster_verified (not evidence_local) and ALL scoreable cells null per the SKILL's sparse-data-trap discipline. Doyle's only police-related public-facing material is on her campaign Safe Neighborhoods page (https://courtneydoyle.com/issues/safe-neighborhoods/), where she lists as her accomplishments funding to 'increase funding and pay for Norfolk's public safety team members' and 'increased police traffic enforcement' — that is budget-action-claimed-as-accomplishment, which the SKILL.md sparse-data discipline (fxbg-2026-06.json precedent) explicitly says does NOT clear the affirmative-advocacy high bar for public_justice q0 (routine governance → null). Without that one cell, the public-facing record on the rubric is genuinely empty, and a 100/100 from a 1-TRUE/0-FALSE denominator would be misleading. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (Catholic parish membership is identity, NOT evidence of a stance — do not infer), biblical_marriage (any cell — no Pride-vote position located; her issue pages make no marriage/sexuality references), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — on schools she voted with the unanimous 8/26/2025 council approval of the $211M Maury HS rebuild and praises 'the appropriate consolidation of schools that is being determined by the Norfolk School Board,' but says nothing on choice/parental rights/CRT-SOGI), christian_liberty (any cell — no profession of Christ; no public-square Christian symbols or prayer statements), economic_stewardship q2 (no BBA/deficit framing; her economic-development page uses 'progressive ideas on incentives' language, no deficit-opposition), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice (any cell — see above), refuse_state_overreach (any cell), border_immigration (any cell), self_defense (any cell). Counter-archetype context: Catholic / Sentara executive / ODU MBA / nonpartisan — moderate-technocrat profile with a thin RESOLUTE-rubric footprint. Do not infer Catholic doctrinal alignment on life/marriage from parish membership.
